steps.title: Spot the pattern that weakens the claim
steps.content: The student claims FTAs increase growth rates. Evidence that would weaken this claim must show growth rates were higher before joining an FTA than after—the opposite of what the claim predicts.

steps.title: Match the pattern to the choices
steps.content: Choice B presents this exact pattern for Nicaragua: the growth rate before CAFTA-DR exceeded the growth rate after CAFTA-DR, directly contradicting the student's claim. The other choices show patterns supporting FTA benefits (positive post-FTA growth, positive reversals, or explicitly higher post-FTA rates), all supporting rather than weakening the claim. That matches choice B — B is correct.
